title: Star versus snowflake schema
description: How a denormalized star trades storage for query simplicity while a normalized snowflake trades joins for reuse.
body: Use when shaping dimensions around a fact table. A star schema keeps each dimension denormalized into one wide table, so queries join the fact to flat dimensions simply; a snowflake normalizes dimensions into sub-tables, saving storage and easing maintenance at the cost of extra joins. Do not snowflake by reflex — on columnar warehouses the storage savings rarely justify the join complexity. Actions: prefer star for query simplicity, snowflake only where a large dimension is widely reused or volatile. Validation signals include join counts matching the chosen schema and a query plan without surprise fan-out. Risk boundary: over-normalized dimensions can fan out and double-count measures; if a join multiplies fact rows, treat the schema as the cause before patching SQL.
